text: We are going to launch the new product in Q3.
But what will we do if something changes?
Backup plan
Setback
Delay
Postpone
Reschedule
In advance
Ahead of schedule
Behind schedule
If the launch is delayed, we will postpone it.
If we are behind schedule, we will use the backup plan.
If it rains on launch day, we will move online.
If the numbers are strong, we will expand.
Thanks for the launch calendar, Emmanuele. It looks clear. Now the question the board always asks: what is your backup plan if things change?
Good question. The normal plan is fixed: we are going to launch in Q3, on schedule. But I have a Rain Plan too. If the product approval is late, we will postpone the launch by one month.
That is sensible. And if you are behind schedule in October?
If we are behind schedule, we will start with the private market first, and we will reschedule the congress for November.
Good. And the launch event is outside. What about the weather?
We thought about that too. If it rains on launch day, we will move the event online. Nothing stops.
I like this. A plan with a backup plan. One more: what if the numbers are strong?
Then it is good news. If the numbers are strong, we will expand the rollout to two more regions, ahead of schedule.
The normal plan is to launch in Q3, on schedule.
If the launch is delayed, we will use the backup plan.
If it rains on launch day, we will move the event online.
If we are behind schedule, we will postpone the congress.
If the numbers are strong, we will expand, ahead of schedule.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Marcus from the Global team. Quick one before Thursday. On Thursday we want to hear your Rain Plan for the Q3 launch. Two things, keep it simple. First, the normal plan: what happens if everything goes well, the standard rollout. And second, the backup plan: if the launch is delayed, what will you do? We know things change, so we want to see that you are ready. If you have a good backup plan, the Global team will feel confident. Send the slide in advance if you can. Thanks, and talk on Thursday.

Okay, let me close the launch part. Here is where we landed. The normal plan is clear: we are going to launch in Q3, on schedule. But we agreed on a strong backup plan. If the product approval is late, we will postpone the launch by one month, and we will reschedule the congress for November. If we are behind schedule in October, we will start with the private market first. And if it rains on launch day, we will move the event online. Emmanuele will send the updated Rain Plan slide by Friday, in advance of the board meeting. Good work, everyone. A plan with a backup plan is a plan we can trust.

The normal plan is to launch in Q3, on schedule. But if the product approval is late, we will postpone the launch by one month. If we are behind schedule, we will start with the private market first. If it rains on launch day, we will move the event online. And if the numbers are strong, we will expand the rollout, ahead of schedule.
